person.name: Eudokia Komnene
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Eudokia Komnene or Eudocia Comnena (Greek: Ευδοκία Κομνηνή, Eudokia Komnēnē), (c. 1150 or 1152 – c. 1203) was a niece of Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos, and wife of William VIII of Montpellier. Eudokia was a daughter of the sebastokratōr Isaac Komnenos by his second wife, Irene Synadene. Her father was a son of Emperor John II Komnenos and Piroska of Hungary, the daughter of King Ladislaus I of Hungary. Her sister Theodora Komnene married King Baldwin III of Jerusalem and was afterwards the lover of Andronikos I Komnenos. Her older half-sister Maria Komnene married King Stephen IV of Hungary. Eudokia Komnene was sent to Provence by Manuel in 1174 to be betrothed to King Alfonso II of Aragon, but, on her arrival, she found that he had just married Sancha of Castile. As the troubadour Peire Vidal put it, he had preferred a poor Castilian maid to the emperor Manuel's golden camel. After much indecision she married William VIII of Montpellier in 1179, having made it a condition (to which all male citizens of Montpellier were required to swear) that their firstborn child, boy or girl, would succeed him in the lordship of Montpellier. Eudokia was sometimes described by contemporaries, including the troubadours Folquet de Marselha and Guiraut de Bornelh, as an empress (Occitan emperairitz) and was commonly said to be a daughter of the emperor Manuel, which has led to some confusion among modern authors about her family links. Other sources, such as Guillaume de Puylaurens, correctly identify her as Manuel's niece. William and Eudokia had one daughter, Marie of Montpellier, born in 1181 or 1182. In 1187 William divorced her (because she encouraged the advances of Folquet de Marselha, according to the Biographies des Troubadours; because William wanted a male heir, according to documents likely to be more reliable). Eudokia was thereafter held at the monastery of Aniane. She died about 1203, shortly before her daughter's third marriage to King Peter II of Aragon. Sources Biographies des troubadours ed. J. Boutière, A.-H. Schutz (Paris: Nizet, 1964) pp. 476–481. Stanislaw Stronski, Le troubadour Folquet de Marseille (Krakow: Académie des Sciences, 1910) pp. 156–158. Ruth V. Sharman. The Cansos and Sirventes of the Troubadour Giraut de Borneil. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1989. ISBN 0-521-25635-6, p. 59. Duvernoy, Jean (1976), Guillaume de Puylaurens, Chronique 1145-1275: Chronica magistri Guillelmi de Podio Laurentii, CNRS, ISBN 2910352064 , pp. 62–63. Bibliography W. Hecht. 'Zur Geschichte der "Kaiserin" von Montpellier, Eudoxia Komnena' in Revue des études byzantines, Vol. 26 (1968), pp. 161–169. K. Varzos. Ē genealogia tōn Komnēnōn, (Thessalonica, 1984) Vol. 2, pp. 346–359. Retrieved from "http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eudokia_Komnene"
LESS



5.         ISAAKIOS Komnenos ([1115]-[1154/74]).  Niketas Choniates names "Alexius, Andronicus et…Isaacius" as the three brothers of Emperor Manuel, stating that the first two died before their father[336].  In an earlier passage, Niketas Choniates names "Isaacius…Manuelis frater sebastocrator".  William of Tyre records the campaign at “Attaliam...provinciæ Pamphyliæ metropolis” in which “dominus imperator duo de filiis eius Alexius...primogenitus et natu secundus Andronicus” both died, adding that the emperor ordered “natu tertiam Isaacium” to return their bodies to Constantinople where they were both buried.  He was passed over by his father who nominated his younger brother Manuel as his successor.

g)         EVDOKIA Komnene .  The primary source which confirms her parentage has not been identified.

m secondly (1179) GUELFO [Paganello] di Porcaria, from Siena.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Eudokia Komnene於1160年出生在دولتْ علیّه عثمانیّه易斯坦波爾易斯坦波爾時，她的父親Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos42歲，母親Marie Princess of Antioch15歲。她和Guillaume VIII de Montpellier至少有2個女兒。她去世於1202年11月4日在塞爾維亞伏伊伏丁那享年42歲，埋葬在法國阿希迪瑟 德 蒙培利也。

nameMeanings.name: Eudokia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of the New Testament Greek names Eudoxia ‘of good appearance’ and Eudokia ‘wellbeing, comfort’. The Byzantine empress Eudocia ( d. c.460 ) was the wife of the emperor Theodosius II . She was a devout Christian, of humble origin, and after the emperor's death in 450 she retired to Jerusalem and spent the last ten years of her life in quiet contemplation.

en.lifeSummary: When Eudokia Komnene was born in 1160, in İstanbul, İstanbul, Ottoman Empire, her father, Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos, was 42 and her mother, Marie Princess of Antioch, was 15. She had at least 2 daughters with Guillaume VIII de Montpellier. She died on 4 November 1202, in Vojvodina, Serbia, at the age of 42, and was buried in Archdiocese of Montpellier, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eudokia Komnene est née en 1160, à İstanbul, İstanbul, Empire ottoman, son père, Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Marie Princess of Antioch, avait 15 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 filles avec Guillaume VIII de Montpellier. Elle est décédée le 4 novembre 1202, à Vojvodina, Serbie, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterrée à Archidiocèse de Montpellier, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eudokia Komnene 1160 in İstanbul, İstanbul, Osmanisches Reich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos 42 und ihre Mutter Marie Princess of Antioch 15. Sie hatte mit Guillaume VIII de Montpellier mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 4. November 1202 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Vojvodina, Serbien und wurde in Archidiocèse de Montpellier, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eudokia Komnene è nata il nel 1160 nel İstanbul, İstanbul, Impero ottomano, sua padre, Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Marie Princess of Antioch, aveva 15 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figlie con Guillaume VIII de Montpellier. Morì il 4 novembre 1202, nel Vojvodina, Serbien, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolta a Archidiocèse de Montpellier, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Eudokia Komneneが1160年にオスマン帝国イスタンブルイスタンブルで生まれた時，父Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenosは42歳，母Marie Princess of Antiochは15歳であった。彼女はGuillaume VIII de Montpellierとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1202年11月4日にセルビアボジボディナで42歳で死去し，フランスアーチディオクeゼ ド モントペリールにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eudokia Komnene(이)가 1160년 دولتْ علیّه عثمانیّه 이스탠불 이스탠불에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie Princess of Antioch(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 Guillaume VIII de Montpellier(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1202년 11월 4일 세르비아 보제이보딘아에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 아치디오ce스 데 몽펠리어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eudokia Komnene nasceu em 1160, em İstanbul, İstanbul, Império Otomano, seu pai, Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Marie Princess of Antioch, tinha 15 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Guillaume VIII de Montpellier. Ela faleceu em 4 de novembro de 1202, em Vojvodina, Sérvia, com 42 anos, e foi sepultada em Archidiocèse de Montpellier, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eudokia Komnene родилась в 1160 г. в местности истанбул, истанбул, Османская империя, её отцу по имени Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Marie Princess of Antioch, 15. У неё было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Guillaume VIII de Montpellier. Она умерла 4 ноября 1202 г. в местности фоджводина, Сербия в возрасте 42 лет и была похоронена в местности арчидиокeс де монтпеллиир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eudokia Komnene nació en 1160, en İstanbul, İstanbul, Imperio otomano, su papá, Byzantine Emperor Manuel I Komnenos, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Marie Princess of Antioch, tenía 15 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Guillaume VIII de Montpellier. Murió el 4 de noviembre de 1202, en Vojvodina, Serbia, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultada en Archidiocèse de Montpellier, Francia.
